# Project brief — medieval-manuscript review

Critically edit a single passage from the Old English Bede
(London, British Library, Cotton Vespasian A.i, f.23v) and
build out two source-provenance chains for the patristic
quotations it cites.

## Goals

1. Transcribe f.23v from the IIIF facsimile with full editorial apparatus.
2. Trace the quote "haec autem omnia tanquam in speculo" (Aug. _Conf._ X)
   back to the Latin Augustinian source via Bede's chain of intermediaries.
3. Trace the quote "uerbum caro factum est" (Joh. 1:14) through the
   Vulgate / Vetus Latina witnesses Bede consulted.
4. Produce a draft critical edition of the passage with apparatus criticus.

## Inputs

- inputs/archival/MS_Vespasian_A_i_f23v.meta.json — IIIF sidecar
- inputs/context/project_brief.md (this file)
